name: Orzo Portobello
recipeIngredient: Salt and crushed red pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Reconstitute sundried tomatoes in a bowl with about 1 cup of water, rinsing them first.
2. Wash portobello mushrooms and remove stems. Place mushroom caps in a dish and sprinkle with herbs.
3. Pour white wine over the mushroom caps to marinate.
4. Coarsely chop the mushroom stems and mince garlic.
5. In a nonstick pan, sauté garlic and mushroom stems in tomato water or wine.
6. Drain tomatoes, reserving liquid, and chop coarsely (no need for full softening).
7. Add chopped mushrooms to the pan edges and pour tomato water over them. Cover and simmer until mushrooms are cooked (about 12 minutes).
8. Remove cooked mushroom caps to plates.
9. Pour remaining marinating wine into the pan and add capers. Cook and stir until reduced.
10. Drain orzo and toss with the mixture. Serve with mushroom caps, optionally garnished with toasted pine nuts.
